content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation PPP of the integers [1,N][1, N][1,N].

Perform the following operation sequence:


For integers i=1,2,...Ni = 1, 2, ...Ni=1,2,...N (in this order), perform one of the following actions:

Choose an integer XXX such that 1‚Č§X<N1 \le X < N1‚Č§X<N, and either PX=iP_X = iPX‚Äč=i or PX+1=iP_{X + 1} = iPX+1‚Äč=i, then swap PXP_XPX‚Äč and PX+1P_{X + 1}PX+1‚Äč.
Choose to do nothing.


Let f(P)f(P)f(P) denote the lexicographically smallest permutation possible after the above operation sequence.

You are given a permutation PPP of the integers [1,N][1, N][1,N].

Find f(P)f(P)f(P).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the permutation PPP
The next line contains NNN integers - P1,P2,...,PNP_1, P_2, ..., P_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,...,PN‚Äč




Output Format
For each test case, print the lexicographically smallest permutation possible with the operation sequence from PPP, i.e. F(P)F(P)F(P).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Here is the required operation sequence to obtain [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3]:

i=1i = 1i=1 : Choose to do nothing
i=2i = 2i=2 : Choose to do nothing
i=3i = 3i=3 : Swap P2P_2P2‚Äč and P3P_3P3‚Äč (P2=3P_2 = 3P2‚Äč=3 before the swap, so it is valid). PPP becomes [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3].

Test Case 2 : It can be shown that lexicographically smaller permutations than [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2] are not possible. Here is how to obtain [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2]:

i=1i = 1i=1 : Swap P2P_2P2‚Äč and P3P_3P3‚Äč (P3=1P_3 = 1P3‚Äč=1 before the swap so it is valid). PPP becomes [3,1,2][3, 1, 2][3,1,2].
i=2i = 2i=2 : Choose to do nothing
i=3i = 3i=3 : Swap P1P_1P1‚Äč and P2P_2P2‚Äč (P1=3P_1 = 3P1‚Äč=3 before the swap, so it is valid). PPP becomes [1,3,2][1, 3, 2][1,3,2].
